markdown.markdown:
This page collects the companion Python labs for **Modern Time Series Analysis and Sequence Learning**. Each lab is a Jupyter notebook stored in the `labs/` folder of the book repository and can be opened directly in Google Colab. Repository: ::: {.callout-tip} ## How to use the labs Use the **Open in Colab** link when reading the online book. In Colab, choose **File $\to$ Save a copy in Drive** before editing. For local work, clone the repository and open the notebooks from the `labs/` folder. ::: ## Lab table | Chapter | Lab | Main focus | |---:|---|---| | 1 | [Why Time Series and Sequence Data Are Different](labs/chapter-01-why-time-series-and-sequences-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-01-why-time-series-and-sequences-lab.ipynb) | simulate white noise, random walks, trend, seasonality, and windowed sequence data | | 2 | [Exploratory Data Analysis for Time Series](labs/chapter-02-exploratory-data-analysis-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-02-exploratory-data-analysis-lab.ipynb) | time plots, smoothing, detrending, differencing, ACF, and seasonal diagnostics | | 3 | [Stationarity, Autocovariance, and Autocorrelation](labs/chapter-03-stationarity-autocovariance-acf-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-03-stationarity-autocovariance-acf-lab.ipynb) | strict/weak stationarity examples, sample autocovariance, ACF, AR(1), MA(1), and random walk | | 4 | [Linear Processes, AR, MA, and ARMA Models](labs/chapter-04-linear-processes-ar-ma-arma-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-04-linear-processes-ar-ma-arma-lab.ipynb) | simulate AR, MA, ARMA processes, root conditions, causality, invertibility, and ACF/PACF behavior | | 5 | [Difference Equations and Dynamic Behavior](labs/chapter-05-difference-equations-dynamic-behavior-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-05-difference-equations-dynamic-behavior-lab.ipynb) | linear recurrences, characteristic roots, damping, oscillation, and explosive dynamics | | 6 | [Forecasting Theory and Best Linear Prediction](labs/chapter-06-forecasting-best-linear-prediction-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-06-forecasting-best-linear-prediction-lab.ipynb) | best linear prediction, forecast variance, AR forecasts, and multi-step forecasting | | 7 | [Estimation, Identification, and Diagnostics](labs/chapter-07-estimation-identification-diagnostics-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-07-estimation-identification-diagnostics-lab.ipynb) | Yule-Walker, likelihood, AIC/BIC, residual ACF, Ljung-Box, and model criticism | | 8 | [ARIMA Models and Differencing](labs/chapter-08-arima-differencing-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-08-arima-differencing-lab.ipynb) | integrated processes, choosing differences, ARIMA fitting, over-differencing, and forecast reconstruction | | 9 | [Seasonal ARIMA and Multiple Seasonality](labs/chapter-09-sarima-multiple-seasonality-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-09-sarima-multiple-seasonality-lab.ipynb) | seasonal differencing, SARIMA fitting, seasonal baselines, and residual diagnostics | | 10 | [State-Space Models and Kalman Filtering](labs/chapter-10-state-space-kalman-filtering-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-10-state-space-kalman-filtering-lab.ipynb) | local-level models, Kalman prediction/update, missing data, smoothing, and state forecasts | | 11 | [Hidden Markov Models and Regime Switching](labs/chapter-11-hidden-markov-regime-switching-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-11-hidden-markov-regime-switching-lab.ipynb) | forward probabilities, Viterbi decoding, Gaussian HMMs, and regime-switching simulations | | 12 | [Spectral Analysis and the Frequency Domain](labs/chapter-12-spectral-analysis-frequency-domain-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-12-spectral-analysis-frequency-domain-lab.ipynb) | sinusoidal processes, aliasing, spectral density, and spectra of AR/MA models | | 13 | [Discrete Fourier Transform, FFT, and Periodogram](labs/chapter-13-dft-fft-periodogram-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-13-dft-fft-periodogram-lab.ipynb) | DFT basis, FFT, periodogram, leakage, tapering, and peak detection | | 14 | [Filtering and Wavelet Transform](labs/chapter-14-filtering-wavelets-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-14-filtering-wavelets-lab.ipynb) | linear filters, frequency response, differencing filters, Haar wavelets, and denoising | | 15 | [Forecast Evaluation and Backtesting](labs/chapter-15-forecast-evaluation-backtesting-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-15-forecast-evaluation-backtesting-lab.ipynb) | rolling-origin validation, baseline comparisons, horizon-wise metrics, intervals, and leakage checks | | 16 | [Automated Forecasting and Prophet-Type Additive Models](labs/chapter-16-automated-forecasting-prophet-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-16-automated-forecasting-prophet-lab.ipynb) | trend, changepoints, Fourier seasonality, holiday effects, Prophet-style models, and tuning | | 17 | [Feature-Based Machine Learning for Time Series](labs/chapter-17-feature-based-machine-learning-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-17-feature-based-machine-learning-lab.ipynb) | lag features, rolling features, calendar variables, Fourier features, tree models, and validation | | 18 | [Probabilistic Forecasting and Uncertainty Quantification](labs/chapter-18-probabilistic-forecasting-uncertainty-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-18-probabilistic-forecasting-uncertainty-lab.ipynb) | prediction intervals, quantile loss, conformal prediction, calibration, and risk decisions | | 19 | [Neural Networks for Time Series](labs/chapter-19-neural-networks-for-time-series-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-19-neural-networks-for-time-series-lab.ipynb) | sliding windows, scaling, MLP forecasting, PyTorch training, and chronological validation | | 20 | [Convolutional Models for Sequences](labs/chapter-20-convolutional-models-sequences-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-20-convolutional-models-sequences-lab.ipynb) | causal convolution, dilation, receptive fields, TCNs, and PyTorch Conv1d | | 21 | [Recurrent Neural Networks, LSTM, and GRU](labs/chapter-21-rnn-lstm-gru-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-21-rnn-lstm-gru-lab.ipynb) | RNN hidden states, LSTM/GRU gates, sequence-to-one forecasting, and recurrent training | | 22 | [Attention and Transformer Models for Time Series](labs/chapter-22-attention-transformers-time-series-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-22-attention-transformers-time-series-lab.ipynb) | scaled dot-product attention, causal masks, positional encodings, and transformer forecasting | | 23 | [Representation Learning for Time Series](labs/chapter-23-representation-learning-time-series-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-23-representation-learning-time-series-lab.ipynb) | embeddings, PCA, autoencoders, masked reconstruction, contrastive ideas, and anomaly detection | | 24 | [Multivariate, Panel, and Hierarchical Time Series](labs/chapter-24-multivariate-panel-hierarchical-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-24-multivariate-panel-hierarchical-lab.ipynb) | VAR models, coupling, Granger-style prediction, global models, and reconciliation | | 25 | [AI-Assisted Time-Series Modeling](labs/chapter-25-ai-assisted-time-series-modeling-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-25-ai-assisted-time-series-modeling-lab.ipynb) | AI workflow auditing, prompt design, leakage review, code review, and report checking | | 26 | [End-to-End Forecasting Pipeline](labs/chapter-26-end-to-end-forecasting-pipeline-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-26-end-to-end-forecasting-pipeline-lab.ipynb) | forecasting contracts, preprocessing, baselines, validation, intervals, and monitoring | | 27 | [Final Project Studio](labs/chapter-27-final-project-studio-lab.ipynb)
[Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda/blob/main/labs/chapter-27-final-project-studio-lab.ipynb) | project design, validation planning, baselines, diagnostics, rubric alignment, and final reporting | ## Lab design pattern Most labs follow a common workflow: 1. State the modeling question and identify the forecasting information set. 2. Simulate or load a time-series/sequence dataset. 3. Visualize the series, dependence structure, and diagnostics. 4. Fit at least one baseline model before fitting a more advanced model. 5. Evaluate with chronological or rolling-origin validation. 6. Interpret residuals, uncertainty, and failure modes. 7. Use AI assistance only after defining the mathematical task and then verify the output manually. ## Recommended local setup ```bash git clone https://github.com/wanghemath/Book-MachineLearning2sda.git cd Book-MachineLearning2sda python -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate pip install -r requirements.txt jupyter lab ``` Alternatively, use Conda: ```bash conda env create -f environment.yml conda activate ts-sequence-learning jupyter lab ``` ## Responsible AI checklist for labs Before submitting any lab, verify the following. - The train/validation/test split respects time order. - Scaling, imputation, feature engineering, and model selection use only information available at the forecast origin. - Every advanced model is compared with a baseline such as naive, seasonal naive, linear AR, ARIMA, or a simple feature model. - Residuals or forecast errors are examined, not only point metrics. - AI-generated code or explanations are checked against the mathematics and the data-generating process. - The final notebook is reproducible from a clean runtime.
